danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite density and aggression patterns on-screen
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat; player must engage or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, distinctly-designed enemy sprite at floor endpoints
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring sustained combat; progression gate

danger_signals.visual_form: Final boss at floor 50; associated with Black Orb crystal
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate existential threat to the world; final confrontation

danger_signals.visual_form: Magic meter UI indicator turns blue or empty
Five-bar HP display diminishing; numeric overflow counter decreasing
danger_signals.meaning: Incremental threat to player survival; state degradation

danger_signals.visual_form: Separate four-bar ally HP meter depleting
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of combat support; increased vulnerability

safety_signals.visual_form: Magic meter UI filled to maximum
safety_signals.meaning: Peak combat readiness; strong ranged magical attack available
Instant full-screen attack available; temporary power surge

safety_signals.visual_form: Second character sprite following player with visible HP bar
safety_signals.meaning: Combat support active; distributed threat absorption

safety_signals.signal: High HP reserve (multiple bars visible + overflow counter)
safety_signals.visual_form: Full five-bar display plus high numeric counter
safety_signals.meaning: Player is in robust defensive state; can absorb damage
Optional advancement; tactical advantage via level bypass

faction_indicators.faction: The Brave One / Player Character (Alan in JP)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occupies left-to-right scroll progression through Dragon Keep tower

faction_indicators.color_identity: Black/dark tones associated with Black Orb
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Black Orb crystal; dark lord archetype
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Final boss sprite; distinct from common enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dragon Keep floor 50; tower summit; throne/tower hierarchy

faction_indicators.faction: Allied character classes (Amazon, Big Man, Knight, Lizardman, Ninja, Priest, Thief, Wizard)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Class-specific visual differentiation (archer bow, axe, armor variants, magical regalia)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visually distinct from enemy units; maintain PC-aligned palette
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Recruited/rescued throughout tower floors; follow player spatially

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Varied floor-specific enemy types; density increases with progression
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed across floors 1-50; wave patterns increase in upper floors

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Dark Lord Drokmar in control; Black Orb active
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dragon Keep tower structure; dark magical aesthetic; enemy saturation throughout levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All enemies actively hostile; tower is contested space; npcs require rescue

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Floor counter/progress UI; vertical or depth-based level progression; increasing visual complexity/darkness in later floors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy density escalates; boss encounters at intervals; ally recruitment opens new tactical options

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Magic meter color/fill state (blue empty → red partial → full); HP bar configuration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Attack strength varies by magic meter state; magical allies toggle attack capability based on meter; full-screen attacks available via items or HP sacrifice

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Black Orb available for interaction; dual-outcome scenario presentation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Two alternate endings: destroy Orb (hero outcome) or seize it (dark lord outcome); world state consequences branch

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hidden progression path becomes visible/accessible via specific maneuver sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can bypass levels; shortcut through tower; tactical efficiency enabled

environmental_storytelling.example: Dragon Keep is structured as 50 ascending floors; Drokmar's throne at summit; visual progression upward communicates increasing threat proximity and world-state danger escalation
8 boss encounters distributed across 51 floors; major narrative beats occur at intervals; visual marker for progression gates and world-state challenges

8 character class variants available for recruitment; each represents a faction/group rescuing during tower ascent; visual presence of ally indicates changing party composition and world-state alliances
Crystal visual represents Drokmar's power source and world threat; player choice at end (destroy vs. seize) communicates moral/political outcome via gameplay-tied decision
Higher floors feature increased enemy waves; visual saturation correlates to world-state danger near tower summit; environmental difficulty slope matches narrative tension

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Performs strong ranged magical attack combined with melee; magical allies also execute ranged attacks; high offensive output

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Magic meter partial (red but not full)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Performs weak ranged magical attack combined with melee; reduced effectiveness
Performs melee-only attack; magical allies refuse to attack; minimum combat effectiveness state
Attack regardless of magic meter state; decoupled from resource management; reliable damage output
Performs strong ranged magical attack; synchronized with player's offensive power
Refuses to attack; only performs actions when player initiates and meter permits; dependent on resource state
Follows player diligently; attacks and jumps only when player does; AI synchronization with player input; no independent initiative
Distinct sprite and attack patterns; major threat; progression gate; unique visual presentation vs. regular enemies
Ultimate antagonist; controls Black Orb; guards dual-outcome choice; narrative culmination
Hostile; attack player on sight; waves increase in frequency and complexity at higher floors; threat density correlates to tower height

notes: Magic Sword uses resource management (magic meter) as a core mechanical signal of readiness state. The game communicates world-state primarily through vertical tower progression and enemy density escalation. Faction identity is conveyed via character class visual differentiation rather than explicit color coding. The dual-ending mechanic (destroy vs. seize Black Orb) encodes the player's final political/moral choice into the world-state outcome. Environmental storytelling relies on spatial hierarchy (tower levels) and encounter pacing (boss placement) rather than explicit narrative text or detailed environmental assets.
